description: Leading Operation Epic Fury with Israel. CENTCOM conducting strikes. Trump announced 5-day pause on energy strikes Mar 23. Deal-making orientation with sudden pivots.
strategic_doctrine: Maximum pressure → sudden pivot to negotiation. Shock and awe military doctrine. Regime change through decapitation strikes.
leadership_style: Unpredictable, transactional, deal-maker. Trump makes decisions via Truth Social announcements, bypassing traditional channels.
leadership_temperament: Impulsive deal-maker, rally-around-flag instinct, sensitive to oil prices and stock market reactions
red_lines: US military casualties exceeding public tolerance
Nuclear weapon use by any party
Domestic economic recession from oil shock
historical_behavior_patterns: 2020 Soleimani strike then de-escalation. 2019 pulled back from Iran strike at last minute. Pattern: escalate dramatically then seek deal. Maximum pressure campaign 2018-2020.
primary_objective: Destroy Iran's nuclear program and force regime capitulation or deal
secondary_objectives: Maintain Strait of Hormuz freedom of navigation
constraints: Oil price impact on domestic economy
Allied GCC states being hit by Iranian retaliation
belief_system: American exceptionalism, freedom of navigation, non-proliferation
domestic_constraints: Congressional opposition growing, oil price at $119 hurting consumers, some officials resigning over war

description: Joint Operation Epic Fury partner. Conducting strikes on Iran nuclear/military targets AND 2026 Lebanon War vs Hezbollah. Critically low on missile interceptors.
strategic_doctrine: Begin doctrine (preemptive strike), Dahiya doctrine (disproportionate force), nuclear ambiguity. Security-maximalist.
leadership_style: Security cabinet decision-making, military-intelligence driven, Netanyahu's political survival calculus
leadership_temperament: Security-maximalist, preemptive, willing to act alone, Netanyahu ties political survival to military success
red_lines: Hezbollah rocket saturation of Israeli cities
historical_behavior_patterns: 2006 Lebanon War, 2007 Syrian reactor strike, multiple Gaza operations, shadow war with Iran 2018-present, Oct 7 response. Pattern: massive initial force, difficulty with prolonged campaigns.
primary_objective: Destroy Iran's nuclear program permanently
constraints: Interceptor depletion crisis (Arrow, David's Sling, Iron Dome critically low)
belief_system: Zionism, 'Never Again' doctrine, security as existential necessity
domestic_constraints: War fatigue after Oct 7, hostage situation legacy, coalition politics, reservist strain

description: Under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (age 56, IRGC-installed after father's assassination Feb 28). Fired 500+ missiles and 2000+ drones. 3 demands to end war. REJECTED Trump's claim of talks and 5-day ceasefire — denies any negotiations are taking place. Rejecting ceasefire concept entirely.
strategic_doctrine: Axis of Resistance, forward defense through proxy network, asymmetric warfare, missile saturation strategy. Strategic patience punctuated by massive retaliation.
leadership_style: IRGC-dominated theocratic. Mojtaba Khamenei represents radical messianic-apocalyptic stream. Security apparatus and ideology fully fused.
leadership_temperament: Radical IRGC-aligned hardliner. 'His father on steroids' — deeper IRGC ties, less diplomatic experience, eschatological worldview
red_lines: Attack on energy infrastructure (already crossed — Asaluyeh)
historical_behavior_patterns: Iran-Iraq War: 8 years of attrition, human wave attacks, accepted massive casualties. 2020 Soleimani: restrained retaliation (missile strike on Al-Asad). 2024 True Promise operations. Pattern: absorb punishment, retaliate asymmetrically, never surrender.
primary_objective: Regime survival while extracting maximum cost from attackers
secondary_objectives: Force US/Israel to accept Iran's 3 demands
belief_system: Shia Islam, Wilayat al-Faqih (Guardianship of the Jurist), Mahdist eschatology, revolutionary ideology. IRGC doctrine of Ashura Warfare (sanctifies sacrifice against injustice) and Alawi Warfare (religious duty over material outcomes).
domestic_constraints: Split between mourners and celebrators after Khamenei death. IRGC consolidating power. Economic devastation. Some civilian areas celebrating strikes on regime infrastructure.

description: Opportunistic supporter of Iran. Providing diplomatic cover via UN veto, potential arms resupply. Distracted by Ukraine but benefits from US overextension and high oil prices.
strategic_doctrine: Multipolarity, anti-Western bloc building, pragmatic opportunism. Support Iran enough to drain US resources without direct confrontation.
leadership_style: Putin's centralized decision-making, KGB-style strategic patience
leadership_temperament: Calculating, pragmatic, exploits Western overextension, avoids direct confrontation with US
red_lines: Direct US military action against Russia
Loss of Syria as a client state
historical_behavior_patterns: Provided S-300 to Iran. Vetoed UN resolutions. Filled oil market gaps under sanctions. Pattern: support adversaries of US without direct involvement.
primary_objective: Weaken US global position while avoiding direct confrontation

known_weaknesses: Limited force projection to Middle East

belief_system: Russian great power nationalism, Orthodox Christian civilization narrative

description: Major Iranian oil buyer. ACTIVELY providing intelligence support via satellite imagery (MizarVision/Jilin-1 releasing US military positions publicly) and possibly BeiDou navigation for Iranian missile guidance. US has accused China of enabling attacks. Benefits from US distraction from Indo-Pacific.
strategic_doctrine: Strategic patience, economic leverage, avoid direct military confrontation. Use the conflict to advance multipolarity narrative.
leadership_style: CCP collective leadership with Xi Jinping's dominant decision-making. Long-term strategic calculus.
leadership_temperament: Patient, calculating, economic-first, avoids military entanglement outside Indo-Pacific
red_lines: Disruption of oil supplies from Persian Gulf
US military presence expansion near China
historical_behavior_patterns: Purchased Iranian oil despite sanctions. Abstained/vetoed at UN. Provided civilian technology with dual-use potential. Never directly intervened militarily in Middle East.
primary_objective: Ensure energy security and strategic advantage from US distraction

known_weaknesses: No force projection to Middle East

belief_system: Chinese Communist Party ideology, national rejuvenation, multipolarity

description: Active combat with Israel since Mar 2 (2026 Lebanon War). IDF hitting 70+ weapons depots. Weakened but fighting. Shia martyrdom ideology.
strategic_doctrine: Resistance against Israel, forward defense of Iran, attrition warfare from prepared positions
leadership_temperament: Ideologically committed, experienced fighters, strong martyrdom culture
primary_objective: Inflict maximum cost on Israel, tie down IDF forces
belief_system: Shia Islam, Wilayat al-Faqih, resistance ideology, martyrdom

description: HOLDING FIRE strategically. Running low on weapons but expanding local production. Declared 'all military options possible' Mar 20. Considering Red Sea naval blockade. Iran holding them in reserve as escalation card.
strategic_doctrine: Strategic patience, Red Sea control, wait for Iran's signal to activate
leadership_temperament: More pragmatic than other Iranian proxies, calculating, preserving forces
red_lines: Strait of Hormuz falling to US control
primary_objective: Maintain Red Sea threat as leverage for Iran's negotiating position
belief_system: Zaydi Shia Islam, anti-imperialism, resistance ideology

description: Diminished post-Oct 7 war. Limited capability but symbolic importance to Axis of Resistance.
primary_objective: Survive and maintain political relevance

description: Iran-aligned militias in Iraq. Actively attacking US bases since Feb 28. Multiple factions with varying Iran loyalty.
strategic_doctrine: Harass US forces in Iraq, threaten US logistics and bases
primary_objective: Force US withdrawal from Iraq through attrition
belief_system: Shia Islam, Iraqi nationalism mixed with Iran loyalty

description: Diplomatic mediation pressure, energy crisis from chokepoint closures, split between Atlanticist and independent foreign policy impulses.
strategic_doctrine: Diplomacy first, protect energy supply, maintain transatlantic alliance while seeking independent voice
primary_objective: End the war through diplomatic means, restore energy supply stability

description: Mediation attempts blocked by US/Russia vetoes. IAEA chief says war can't entirely eliminate Iran's nuclear program.
strategic_doctrine: Multilateral diplomacy, international law, nuclear non-proliferation
primary_objective: Achieve ceasefire and return to diplomatic process

description: US bases on Saudi soil hit by Iranian missiles. Oil infrastructure vulnerable (Abqaiq 2019 precedent). Torn between US alliance and self-preservation. Kuwait/Saudi refineries already hit.
strategic_doctrine: Hedging between US alliance and Iran accommodation, protect oil infrastructure at all costs
primary_objective: Protect energy infrastructure and avoid becoming a primary target

description: US bases hit, gas facilities closed. F-35 operator. Economic hub at risk. Dual hedging between US and regional stability.
strategic_doctrine: Economic security first, military hedging, avoid becoming a primary battleground
primary_objective: Protect economic infrastructure and maintain stability

description: Al Udeid base (CENTCOM forward HQ) hit by Iran. Ras Laffan LNG facility destroyed (17% capacity, $20B/year loss, 3-5 year repair). Mediation role via Al Jazeera and diplomatic channels.
strategic_doctrine: Mediation diplomacy, protect LNG assets, maintain relationships with all sides
primary_objective: End the war before more infrastructure is destroyed

description: NATO member with US base (Incirlik) hit by Iran. Erdogan pursuing independent foreign policy. Regional power with own interests.
strategic_doctrine: Independent regional power, balance NATO obligations with regional ambitions
primary_objective: Expand Turkish influence in post-war Middle East order

description: Oil supply crisis. Brent hit $119. Strait of Hormuz closed. Gulf state refineries damaged. Production decisions critical for global economy.
primary_objective: Maintain oil market stability amid unprecedented disruption

description: Nuclear state sharing border with Iran. Sunni power balancing relations. Potential humanitarian corridor role.
strategic_doctrine: Strategic hedging, protect nuclear assets, balance Iran-US-China relationships
primary_objective: Avoid being drawn into the conflict, maintain nuclear deterrent credibility

description: Press TV, IRGC-affiliated channels, Tasnim News Agency. Broadcasting martyrdom narratives, Minab school strike footage, regime resilience messaging.
primary_objective: Maintain domestic morale, build international sympathy, frame conflict as US aggression

description: Framing Operation Epic Fury as necessary to prevent nuclear Iran. Focus on military success, downplay civilian casualties.
primary_objective: Maintain domestic support for military operations, counter Iranian narrative

description: Al Jazeera, Reuters, AP, satellite imagery analysts. Covering civilian casualties, infrastructure damage, global economic impact. OSINT community tracking military movements.
primary_objective: Report factual developments, hold all parties accountable
